Active during the nineteenth century, Danish painter of the Golden Age whose patriotic, meticulously detailed landscapes of Zealand's rolling hills and coastal views celebrated the Danish countryside with quiet, luminous precision. His work expressed a deeply felt national Romanticism. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: P.S. Krøyer
bio: P.S. Krøyer was a Danish painter associated with Impressionism & Skagen Painters. Active during the nineteenth century, Danish painter and leading figure of the Skagen artists' colony, celebrated for his luminous, sun-filled depictions of coastal life, beach scenes, and the famous 'Summer Evening on Skagen's Southern Beach.' His vibrant palette and plein-air technique brought Scandinavian Impressionism to its peak. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Joaquín Sorolla
bio: Joaquín Sorolla was a Spanish painter associated with Impressionism & Luminism. Active during the nineteenth century, Spanish painter whose virtuoso, sun-drenched beach scenes and garden paintings captured the brilliant Mediterranean light with unmatched luminosity and fluency. His monumental 'Vision of Spain' series for the Hispanic Society of America is a landmark of early 20th-century muralism. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Joaquim Mir
bio: Joaquim Mir was a Spanish painter associated with Post-Impressionism & Modernisme. Active during the early twentieth century, Spanish Catalan painter of the Modernisme movement whose intensely personal, vibrantly colored landscapes of Mallorca and the Priorat region verged on abstraction. His expressive, almost Fauvist use of color made him one of the most original Spanish painters of his generation. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Ramón Casas
bio: Ramón Casas was a Spanish painter associated with Modernisme & Post-Impressionism. Active during the early twentieth century, Spanish Catalan painter and graphic artist of the Modernisme movement, known for his portraits of Barcelona's intellectual elite and atmospheric scenes of Parisian and Spanish urban life. His work documented the cultural renaissance of turn-of-the-century Barcelona with keen observation and modern sensibility. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Eugenio Lucas Velázquez
bio: Eugenio Lucas Velázquez was a Spanish painter associated with Romanticism. Active during the nineteenth century, Spanish Romantic painter known for his vigorous, Goya-inspired scenes of the Inquisition, bullfights, and Spanish popular life rendered with dark, expressive brushwork. His often-violent, fantastical imagery kept the Romantic spirit alive in mid-19th-century Spain. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Eduardo Rosales
bio: Eduardo Rosales was a Spanish painter associated with Realism. Active during the nineteenth century, Spanish realist painter whose historically accurate, quietly dramatic scenes from Spanish history, notably 'The Death of Lucretia,' broke from Romantic bombast toward sober, critical realism. His restrained palette and moral seriousness influenced the next generation of Spanish painters. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Francisco de Goya
bio: Francisco de Goya was a Spanish painter associated with Romanticism & Rococo. Active during the eighteenth century, Spanish painter and printmaker whose unflinching depictions of war, madness, and human folly in works like 'The Third of May 1808' and the 'Black Paintings' anticipated modern art's psychological depth. His profoundly original vision bridged the Old Masters and modernism. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Diego Velázquez
bio: Diego Velázquez was a Spanish painter associated with Baroque. Active during the seventeenth century, Spanish Baroque painter and court artist to Philip IV whose virtuoso handling of paint, complex compositions, and penetrating psychological realism in works like 'Las Meninas' set the highest standard for European painting. His influence extends from Goya to Manet, Picasso, and Bacon. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Kamāl ud-Dīn Behzād
bio: Kamāl ud-Dīn Behzād was a Persian painter associated with Persian miniature & Timurid art. Active during the late medieval period, the preeminent master of Persian miniature painting, Behzād served as head of the royal ateliers in Herat and Tabriz. His sophisticated compositions, expressive figures, and innovative use of space profoundly influenced Islamic painting across Persia, India, and the Ottoman Empire. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Reza Abbasi
bio: Reza Abbasi was a Persian painter associated with Persian miniature & Safavid art. Active during the seventeenth century, the leading court painter of Safavid Iran under Shah Abbas I, Reza Abbasi revolutionized Persian miniature painting with his calligraphic linework, intimate single-figure portraits, and elegant depictions of courtly youth. His distinctive style defined Isfahan school painting for generations. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Basawan
bio: Basawan was an Indian painter associated with Mughal painting. Active during the sixteenth century, One of the most celebrated Mughal miniature painters in Emperor Akbar's atelier, Basawan was renowned for his dynamic compositions, psychological depth in portraiture, and mastery of naturalistic landscape backgrounds. His illustrations for the Hamzanama and Razmnama are considered masterpieces of the Mughal school. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Mansur
bio: Mansur was an Indian painter associated with Mughal painting. Active during the seventeenth century, a master Mughal natural-history painter in the courts of Akbar and Jahangir, Mansur was famed for his extraordinarily precise and vibrant depictions of birds, flowers, and animals. His portrait of the dodo and studies of Siberian cranes are among the most celebrated works of Mughal naturalism. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Nainsukh
bio: Nainsukh was an Indian painter associated with Pahari painting. Active during the eighteenth century, the most innovative Pahari miniature painter of the 18th century, Nainsukh worked for the Raja of Jasrota and developed an extraordinarily naturalistic style marked by psychological insight and subtle observation. His intimate court scenes and portraits broke decisively from earlier conventions of Indian miniature painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Raden Saleh
bio: Raden Saleh was an Indonesian painter associated with Romanticism. Active during the nineteenth century, the pioneering Indonesian Romantic painter, Raden Saleh trained in Europe and blended Javanese aesthetic sensibilities with European academic painting. His dramatic landscapes, hunting scenes, and historical works such as The Arrest of Diponegoro established him as the founding figure of modern Indonesian art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Nakkaş Osman
bio: Nakkaş Osman was an Ottoman painter associated with Ottoman miniature. Active during the sixteenth century, the most prolific Ottoman court miniaturist of the 16th century, Nakkaş Osman served as chief illustrator in Sultan Murad III's workshop. His detailed historical manuscripts, including the Shahnameh and Siyer-i Nebi, established the classical Ottoman miniature style. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Levni
bio: Levni was an Ottoman painter associated with Ottoman miniature & Tulip period. Active during the eighteenth century, the most famous Ottoman miniaturist of the 18th century, Levni revitalized the art of Turkish manuscript illustration with his vibrant colors, graceful figures, and lively depictions of court festivals. His Surname-i Vehbi documenting the 1720 imperial circumcision festival is his masterwork. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Kanō Eitoku
bio: Kanō Eitoku was a Japanese painter associated with Kanō school & Japanese painting & Momoyama period. Active during the sixteenth century, the most influential painter of the Momoyama period, Kanō Eitoku established the monumental Kanō school style of bold ink washes, gold-leaf backgrounds, and massive folding screens. His work for Oda Nobunaga and Toyotomi Hideyoshi defined the visual aesthetic of the Japanese warrior aristocracy. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Kanō Sanraku
bio: Kanō Sanraku was a Japanese painter associated with Kanō school & Japanese painting. Active during the sixteenth century, succeeded Eitoku as head of the Kanō school and helped sustain its dominance into the early Edo period. His powerful screen paintings combining Chinese-inspired brushwork with Japanese decorative traditions bridge the Momoyama and Edo visual cultures. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Nagasawa Rosetsu
bio: Nagasawa Rosetsu was a Japanese painter associated with Maruyama-Shijō school & Japanese painting & Edo period. Active during the eighteenth century, an eccentric and virtuosic Japanese painter of the Edo period, Rosetsu developed a bold, expressive style that broke from the decorum of the Maruyama school. His dynamic ink paintings, often on colossal screens, display an extraordinary freedom of brushwork and inventive composition. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Sakai Hōitsu
bio: Sakai Hōitsu was a Japanese painter associated with Rinpa school & Japanese painting & Edo period. Active during the eighteenth century, the leading Rinpa school painter of the late Edo period, Hōitsu revived and refined the decorative style of Ogata Kōrin. His elegant folding screens and hanging scrolls, combining lavish gold grounds with subtle natural motifs, epitomize the sophisticated Rinpa aesthetic. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Xu Xi
bio: Xu Xi was a Chinese painter associated with Chinese painting & Flower-and-bird painting & Five Dynasties. Active during the late medieval period, a seminal painter of the Five Dynasties period, Xu Xi founded the xieyi (freehand) tradition of Chinese flower-and-bird painting. His spontaneous ink washes and subtle color harmonies contrasted with the courtly precision of his rival Huang Quan, establishing a fundamental polarity in Chinese bird-and-flower painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Li Gonglin
bio: Li Gonglin was a Chinese painter associated with Chinese painting & Literati painting & Song dynasty. Active during the late medieval period, a preeminent literati painter of the Northern Song dynasty, Li Gonglin was celebrated for his baimiao (plain-line) ink paintings of horses, figures, and Buddhist subjects. His refined, archaistic style deeply influenced later Chinese scholar-official painting traditions. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Liang Kai
bio: Liang Kai was a Chinese painter associated with Chinese painting & Zen painting & Southern Song dynasty. Active during the late medieval period, a revolutionary Chan (Zen) Buddhist painter of the Southern Song, Liang Kai abandoned meticulous detail for explosive, abbreviated ink brushwork that captured spiritual essence with minimal strokes. His masterpiece Immortal in Splashed Ink epitomizes the Chan ideal of spontaneous expression. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Miguel Cabrera
bio: Miguel Cabrera was a Mexican painter associated with Mexican Baroque & New Spanish painting. Active during the eighteenth century, the most celebrated Mexican painter of the 18th century, Cabrera produced hundreds of religious works, portraits, and casta paintings documenting colonial racial hierarchies. His skill as a portraitist and his monumental altarpieces made him the leading figure of the New Spanish Baroque. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Saturnino Herrán
bio: Saturnino Herrán was a Mexican painter associated with Mexican art & Indigenism & Symbolism. Active during the early twentieth century, a pivotal figure in early 20th-century Mexican art, Herrán fused Symbolist aesthetics with indigenist themes, depicting Indigenous figures with dignity and monumentality. His series of works on Mexican popular culture and his mural designs directly anticipated the Mexican muralist movement. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Ángel Zárraga
bio: Ángel Zárraga was a Mexican painter associated with Mexican art & Modernism & Muralism. Active during the early twentieth century, a Mexican modernist painter who spent much of his career in France, Zárraga blended Cubist structural elements with Symbolist and classical themes. His mural cycles, including those at the Palacio de Bellas Artes, combine European avant-garde techniques with Mexican subject matter. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: José María Obregón
bio: José María Obregón was a Mexican painter associated with Mexican art & Academic art & History painting. Active during the nineteenth century, a leading Mexican academic painter of the 19th century, Obregón specialized in allegorical and historical compositions that shaped Mexican national identity. His monumental painting The Discovery of Pulque became one of the most recognisable images of Mexican art history. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Victor Meirelles
bio: Victor Meirelles was a Brazilian painter associated with Brazilian academic art & History painting. Active during the nineteenth century, the most important Brazilian history painter of the 19th century, Meirelles created the iconic canvas The First Mass in Brazil and the monumental Battle of the Guararapes. His academic training in Paris and his patriotic works established the visual vocabulary of Brazilian national identity. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Pedro Américo
bio: Pedro Américo was a Brazilian painter associated with Brazilian academic art & History painting. Active during the nineteenth century, a preeminent Brazilian academic painter and writer, Américo is best known for his epic canvas Independence or Death, the definitive visual representation of Brazil's independence. His dramatic historical compositions in the grand European academic tradition defined Brazilian official painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Almeida Júnior
bio: Almeida Júnior was a Brazilian painter associated with Brazilian Realism. Active during the nineteenth century, the leading Brazilian realist painter of the 19th century, Almeida Júnior turned away from academic history painting to depict rural life, folk types, and regional customs of São Paulo state. His masterpiece Caipira Picando Fumo is an iconic work of Brazilian art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Eliseu Visconti
bio: Eliseu Visconti was a Brazilian painter associated with Brazilian Impressionism & Art Nouveau. Active during the early twentieth century, the foremost Brazilian Impressionist and Art Nouveau painter, Visconti studied in Paris and brought European modernist currents to Brazil. His decorative panels for the Rio de Janeiro Municipal Theatre and his luminous plein-air landscapes rank among the finest Brazilian works of the Belle Époque. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Prilidiano Pueyrredón
bio: Prilidiano Pueyrredón was an Argentine painter associated with Argentine art & Costumbrismo. Active during the nineteenth century, the first major Argentine painter, Pueyrredón combined a career as an architect, engineer, and painter, producing pioneering costumbrista scenes of rural Argentine life and sensitive portraits of the elite. His work documents the social fabric of 19th-century Argentina with both intimacy and scope. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Eduardo Sívori
bio: Eduardo Sívori was an Argentine painter associated with Argentine art & Realism. Active during the nineteenth century, a founding figure of Argentine naturalist painting, Sívori trained in Paris under Jean-Léon Gérôme and introduced academic realism to Buenos Aires. His socially conscious works, especially The Awakening of the Servant, brought French realist sensibilities to Argentine subject matter. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Ernesto de la Cárcova
bio: Ernesto de la Cárcova was an Argentine painter associated with Argentine art & Realism. Active during the early twentieth century, the most celebrated Argentine social realist painter, de la Cárcova is remembered for his masterpiece Without Bread and Without Work, an iconic depiction of working-class hardship. He later became a pioneering director of the Argentine Academy of Fine Arts, shaping the country's art education. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Cornelius Krieghoff
bio: Cornelius Krieghoff was a Canadian painter associated with Canadian art & Genre painting. Active during the nineteenth century, the most popular Canadian painter of the 19th century, Krieghoff created vivid, anecdotal scenes of rural Quebecois and Indigenous life that defined early Canadian visual culture. His winter landscapes and habitant genre scenes remain among the most beloved works in Canadian art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Paul Kane
bio: Paul Kane was a Canadian painter associated with Canadian art & Ethnographic painting. Active during the nineteenth century, Canada's pioneering ethnographic painter, Kane travelled thousands of miles across the Great Lakes, the Prairies, and the Pacific Northwest documenting Indigenous peoples and landscapes. His hundreds of field sketches and oil paintings provide an invaluable visual record of pre-confederation Indigenous cultures. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Tom Thomson
bio: Tom Thomson was a Canadian painter associated with Canadian art & Group of Seven & Post-Impressionism. Active during the early twentieth century, the iconic Canadian landscape painter whose bold, vibrant sketches of Algonquin Park wilderness inspired the Group of Seven. Thomson's tragic drowning at age thirty-nine elevated him to a mythic status in Canadian art, and his canvases such as The Jack Pine and The West Wind define the Canadian landscape tradition. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Marc-Aurèle de Foy Suzor-Coté
bio: Marc-Aurèle de Foy Suzor-Coté was a Canadian painter associated with Canadian art & Impressionism & Post-Impressionism. Active during the early twentieth century, a leading Canadian Impressionist who trained in Paris at the École des Beaux-Arts, Suzor-Coté painted luminous landscapes of Quebec and dynamic winter scenes. His thick impasto, vibrant palette, and sculptural works made him the most original Canadian painter of his generation. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Ozias Leduc
bio: Ozias Leduc was a Canadian painter associated with Canadian art & Symbolism & Post-Impressionism. Active during the nineteenth century, a profoundly original Quebecois painter and stained-glass artist, Leduc infused his still lifes, portraits, and religious works with Symbolist mystery and quiet luminosity. His subtle colour harmonies and spiritual sensibility deeply influenced his pupil Paul-Émile Borduas and the Refus global movement. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Tom Roberts
bio: Tom Roberts was an Australian painter associated with Australian art & Heidelberg School & Impressionism. Active during the nineteenth century, the driving force behind the Heidelberg School, Roberts brought plein-air Impressionist techniques from Europe to Australia and created iconic works documenting Australian rural life. His paintings Shearing the Rams and The Breakaway captured the ethos of the emerging Australian nation. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Arthur Streeton
bio: Arthur Streeton was an Australian painter associated with Australian art & Heidelberg School & Impressionism. Active during the early twentieth century, the most celebrated landscape painter of the Heidelberg School, Streeton captured the golden light and vast scale of the Australian bush with dazzling Impressionist technique. His masterpiece Fire's On and panoramic views of Sydney Harbour remain defining works of Australian painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Charles Conder
bio: Charles Conder was an Australian painter associated with Australian art & Heidelberg School & Impressionism. Active during the early twentieth century, a key member of the Heidelberg School, Conder brought a decorative, lyrical sensibility to Australian Impressionism with his elegant compositions and refined colour sense. His work at the artists' camp at Eaglemont with Roberts and Streeton laid the foundation of Australian landscape painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Frederick McCubbin
bio: Frederick McCubbin was an Australian painter associated with Australian art & Heidelberg School. Active during the nineteenth century, a founding member of the Heidelberg School, McCubbin specialized in poignant scenes of pioneer life in the Australian bush, imbuing his canvases with a gentle melancholy and narrative depth. His triptych The Pioneer is one of the most cherished works in Australian art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: John Glover
bio: John Glover was an Australian painter associated with Australian art & Romanticism & Landscape painting. Active during the nineteenth century, a successful English landscape painter who emigrated to Van Diemen's Land at age sixty-three, Glover adapted his Claudian style to the Australian landscape with remarkable freshness. His luminous views of Tasmanian wilderness are among the earliest and finest Australian colonial paintings. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Károly Lotz
bio: Károly Lotz was a Hungarian painter associated with Hungarian art & Academic art & Romanticism. Active during the nineteenth century, the most prolific Hungarian academic painter of the 19th century, Lotz decorated Budapest's grandest buildings, including the Hungarian Parliament and the Opera House, with monumental frescoes. His versatile mastery of historical, religious, and allegorical subjects shaped the visual identity of late Habsburg Hungary. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Gyula Benczúr
bio: Gyula Benczúr was a Hungarian painter associated with Hungarian art & Academic art & History painting. Active during the nineteenth century, the foremost Hungarian history painter of the late 19th century, Benczúr studied under Piloty in Munich and created monumental canvases of Magyar history with dramatic realism. His painting The Baptism of Vajk and portraits of aristocracy made him the most sought-after Hungarian painter of his era. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Miklós Barabás
bio: Miklós Barabás was a Hungarian painter associated with Hungarian art & Romanticism & Biedermeier. Active during the nineteenth century, the leading Hungarian portrait painter of the 19th century, Barabás painted over two thousand portraits of the Hungarian political and cultural elite, including Lajos Kossuth and István Széchenyi. His Biedermeier sensibility and refined technique defined Hungarian portraiture for a generation. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Bertalan Székely
bio: Bertalan Székely was a Hungarian painter associated with Hungarian art & Romanticism & History painting. Active during the nineteenth century, the most important Hungarian Romantic history painter, Székely created emotionally charged depictions of the Magyar past that resonated with Hungarian nationalism. His monumental works such as The Discovery of the Holy Crown and The Battle of Mohács are cornerstones of Hungarian historical painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Piotr Michałowski
bio: Piotr Michałowski was a Polish painter associated with Polish art & Romanticism. Active during the nineteenth century, the greatest Polish Romantic painter, Michałowski is famed for his dynamic battle scenes, equestrian portraits, and expressive oil sketches of Polish soldiers. His vigorous brushwork and dramatic chiaroscuro anticipated Realism and made him a towering figure of Polish 19th-century painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Józef Brandt
bio: Józef Brandt was a Polish painter associated with Polish art & History painting & Munich School. Active during the nineteenth century, the leading Polish historical painter of the Munich School, Brandt specialized in dramatic battle scenes and genre works set in the 17th-century Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth. His meticulously researched canvases celebrating Polish military history made him enormously popular at home and abroad. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Aleksander Gierymski
bio: Aleksander Gierymski was a Polish painter associated with Polish art & Realism & Impressionism. Active during the nineteenth century, the most accomplished Polish painter of the Realist and early Impressionist schools, Gierymski captured the luminous atmosphere of Warsaw streets and Jewish quarter life with extraordinary sensitivity. His masterpiece The Feast of Trumpets ranks among the finest 19th-century Polish paintings. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Leon Wyczółkowski
bio: Leon Wyczółkowski was a Polish painter associated with Polish art & Realism & Impressionism. Active during the nineteenth century, a leading Polish painter who evolved from academic realism through Impressionism to a personal symbolic style, Wyczółkowski excelled in landscapes, still lifes, and portraits. His late floral still lifes with bold colour and expressive brushwork are masterpieces of Polish modernism. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Václav Brožík
bio: Václav Brožík was a Czech painter associated with Czech art & Academic art & History painting. Active during the nineteenth century, the most internationally successful Czech history painter of the 19th century, Brožík worked in Paris and Prague, winning medals at the Salon for large-scale canvases of Czech history. His monumental Defenestration of Prague and Hus at the Council of Constance are defining works of Czech academic painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Jakub Schikaneder
bio: Jakub Schikaneder was a Czech painter associated with Czech art & Romanticism. Active during the nineteenth century, a celebrated Czech painter known for his melancholy, atmospheric scenes of solitary female figures in twilight interiors and snow-covered urban landscapes. Schikaneder's personal, introspective style and subtle tonal harmonies distinguish him from the mainstream of Czech 19th-century painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Jan Preisler
bio: Jan Preisler was a Czech painter associated with Czech art & Symbolism & Art Nouveau. Active during the early twentieth century, a leading Czech Symbolist and Art Nouveau painter, Preisler created evocative mythological and allegorical works blending decorative elegance with psychological depth. His monumental murals and sensitive figure paintings mark the transition from 19th-century academicism to Czech modernism. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Antonín Slavíček
bio: Antonín Slavíček was a Czech painter associated with Czech art & Impressionism. Active during the early twentieth century, the foremost Czech Impressionist landscape painter, Slavíček brought French Impressionist techniques to the Bohemian countryside, capturing its moods with vigorous broken brushwork and luminous colour. His premature death at forty cut short the most promising career in early Czech modernist painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Nils Kreuger
bio: Nils Kreuger was a Swedish painter associated with Swedish art & Impressionism & National Romanticism. Active during the nineteenth century, a leading Swedish Impressionist and National Romantic painter, Kreuger developed a distinctive pointillist technique applied to Swedish rural landscapes and animal scenes. His decorative, rhythmically structured paintings of horses and cows in open pastures are unique in Scandinavian art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Richard Bergh
bio: Richard Bergh was a Swedish painter associated with Swedish art & National Romanticism & Symbolism. Active during the nineteenth century, a central figure of Swedish National Romantic painting, Bergh combined a Symbolist sensibility with a deep attachment to Nordic landscape and folk culture. As director of the Swedish National Museum, he also profoundly shaped the institutional framework of Swedish art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Hanna Pauli
bio: Hanna Pauli was a Swedish painter associated with Swedish art & Impressionism. Active during the nineteenth century, One of Sweden's foremost women painters of the turn of the century, Pauli trained in Paris and created sophisticated portraits, genre scenes, and still lifes with a refined Impressionist touch. Her portrait of the artist colony at Grez-sur-Loing is a treasured document of Scandinavian artistic life. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Eero Järnefelt
bio: Eero Järnefelt was a Finnish painter associated with Finnish art & Realism & Naturalism. Active during the nineteenth century, a leading Finnish Realist painter associated with the Golden Age of Finnish art, Järnefelt is best known for his monumental naturalist landscapes and depictions of rural labour. His canvas Under the Yoke, portraying Finnish peasants, became a symbol of Finnish national identity. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Pekka Halonen
bio: Pekka Halonen was a Finnish painter associated with Finnish art & National Romanticism & Symbolism. Active during the early twentieth century, a master of Finnish National Romantic landscape painting, Halonen studied in Paris with Puvis de Chavannes and developed a monumental, decorative style celebrating the Finnish winter and forest. His winter landscapes and scenes of domestic life are icons of the Golden Age of Finnish art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Juho Rissanen
bio: Juho Rissanen was a Finnish painter associated with Finnish art & Realism. Active during the early twentieth century, a prominent Finnish Realist painter of rural life, Rissanen documented the lives of working people in eastern Finland with powerful, monumental compositions. His murals for public buildings and his deeply humanistic paintings of peasants and fishermen are among the finest in Finnish art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Lars Hertervig
bio: Lars Hertervig was a Norwegian painter associated with Norwegian art & Romanticism & Landscape painting. Active during the nineteenth century, the most original Norwegian landscape painter of the 19th century, Hertervig created visionary, intensely personal views of the coastal landscape of western Norway. His luminous, almost hallucinatory depictions of pine forests and fjords, produced largely in obscurity and poverty, were rediscovered as precursors of Expressionism. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Frits Thaulow
bio: Frits Thaulow was a Norwegian painter associated with Norwegian art & Impressionism & Realism. Active during the nineteenth century, the most internationally successful Norwegian Impressionist painter, Thaulow specialized in luminous landscape scenes of rivers, snow, and mills, often working in France and Denmark. His intimate, atmospheric depictions of water in motion earned him the nickname the painter of running water. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Halfdan Egedius
bio: Halfdan Egedius was a Norwegian painter associated with Norwegian art & Realism. Active during the early twentieth century, a brilliant Norwegian painter who died tragically young at twenty-two, Egedius produced richly coloured, psychologically acute scenes of rural Norwegian life and characters from Norse mythology. His small but exquisite body of work, especially The Village Smithy, ranks among the finest of Norwegian realism. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Nicolae Grigorescu
bio: Nicolae Grigorescu was a Romanian painter associated with Romanian art & Impressionism & Realism. Active during the nineteenth century, the founding father of modern Romanian painting, Grigorescu introduced Impressionism and plein-air painting to Romania after training in Paris at Barbizon. His iconic depictions of Romanian peasants, landscapes, and religious works transformed Romanian art and inspired generations of followers. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Ștefan Luchian
bio: Ștefan Luchian was a Romanian painter associated with Romanian art & Impressionism & Post-Impressionism. Active during the early twentieth century, Romania's greatest modern painter, Luchian infused Impressionist and Post-Impressionist techniques with a profoundly personal, lyrical sensibility. His luminous still lifes of flowers and vibrant peasant scenes, painted despite a devastating neurological illness, are among the most cherished works in Romanian art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Theodor Aman
bio: Theodor Aman was a Romanian painter associated with Romanian art & Academic art & History painting. Active during the nineteenth century, a leading figure of 19th-century Romanian art, Aman founded the National School of Fine Arts in Bucharest and introduced academic history painting to Romania. His dramatic historical canvases, portraits, and genre scenes established the institutional foundations of modern Romanian painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Zahari Zograf
bio: Zahari Zograf was a Bulgarian painter associated with Bulgarian art & National Revival & Icon painting. Active during the nineteenth century, the most important Bulgarian painter of the National Revival period, Zahari Zograf revolutionized Orthodox iconography by introducing realistic portraiture and everyday details into church frescoes. His murals at the Rila Monastery and the Transfiguration Monastery are the crowning achievements of 19th-century Bulgarian religious painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Nadežda Petrović
bio: Nadežda Petrović was a Serbian painter associated with Serbian art & Expressionism & Fauvism. Active during the early twentieth century, the most important Serbian woman painter of the early 20th century, Petrović brought Fauvist intensity and Expressionist emotion to Balkan subjects and landscapes. Her bold colour, vigorous brushwork, and patriotic spirit made her a pioneering figure of Yugoslav modernist painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Vlaho Bukovac
bio: Vlaho Bukovac was a Croatian painter associated with Croatian art & Academic art & Impressionism. Active during the nineteenth century, the most influential Croatian painter of the late 19th century, Bukovac studied in Paris and introduced Impressionist techniques to the Balkans. His portrait of the Croatian national poet The Singer earned him acclaim, and his later work as a teacher shaped the Zagreb modern art scene. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Rihard Jakopič
bio: Rihard Jakopič was a Slovenian painter associated with Slovenian art & Impressionism. Active during the early twentieth century, the leading figure of Slovenian Impressionism, Jakopič founded the first Slovenian artists' colony and introduced modern European painting currents to Ljubljana. His luminous landscapes of Slovenia and his role in founding the National Gallery of Slovenia make him the central figure of modern Slovenian art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Ivan Grohar
bio: Ivan Grohar was a Slovenian painter associated with Slovenian art & Impressionism. Active during the early twentieth century, a leading Slovenian Impressionist, Grohar created the most iconic painting in Slovenian art, the Sower, which became a national symbol. His richly textured landscapes and scenes of rural life captured the essence of the Slovenian countryside with modernist verve. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Vilhelms Purvītis
bio: Vilhelms Purvītis was a Latvian painter associated with Latvian art & Landscape painting & Realism. Active during the early twentieth century, the founding father of Latvian landscape painting, Purvītis created monumental, atmospheric depictions of Latvian forests, rivers, and snow-covered fields that defined the national romantic landscape tradition. His role as director of the Latvian Art Academy shaped the country's artistic identity. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Janis Rozentāls
bio: Janis Rozentāls was a Latvian painter associated with Latvian art & Realism & Symbolism. Active during the early twentieth century, the most versatile and internationally recognized Latvian painter of his generation, Rozentāls worked across Realism, Symbolism, and Art Nouveau in portraits, landscapes, and allegories. His paintings of Latvian folklore and everyday life are foundational works of Latvian national art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Konrad Mägi
bio: Konrad Mägi was an Estonian painter associated with Estonian art & Expressionism & Post-Impressionism. Active during the early twentieth century, the most original Estonian painter of the early 20th century, Mägi fused Post-Impressionist colour with Expressionist intensity in his vibrantly coloured landscapes of Estonia, the Italian island of Capri, and Norway. His bold, expressive use of colour and simplified forms heralded modernism in Estonian art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Johann Köler
bio: Johann Köler was an Estonian painter associated with Estonian art & Academic art & Realism. Active during the nineteenth century, the first professionally trained Estonian painter, Köler painted portraits of the Russian imperial court and scenes of Estonian peasant life with academic precision. His genre works and his role in founding the Estonian artistic community in Saint Petersburg established him as a pioneer of Estonian national art. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Albert Anker
bio: Albert Anker was a Swiss painter associated with Swiss art & Realism. Active during the nineteenth century, Switzerland's most beloved 19th-century painter, Anker meticulously documented rural Swiss village life in warm, meticulously detailed genre scenes and portraits. His depictions of children, schoolrooms, and domestic interiors are cherished national icons of Swiss cultural memory. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Jean-Étienne Liotard
bio: Jean-Étienne Liotard was a Swiss painter associated with Rococo. Active during the eighteenth century, a celebrated Swiss pastellist and painter known for his remarkably naturalistic portraits and exotic self-portraits in Turkish dress. Liotard travelled through Europe and the Ottoman Empire, and his meticulously rendered pastel portraits of European royalty and Enlightenment figures earned him international fame. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Johann Ludwig Aberli
bio: Johann Ludwig Aberli was a Swiss painter associated with Swiss art & Landscape painting & Rococo. Active during the eighteenth century, a pioneering Swiss landscape painter and printmaker, Aberli developed the tinted outline etching technique that made his panoramic views of the Swiss Alps widely disseminated. His travel landscapes initiated the tradition of Swiss tourist topography and deeply influenced later Alpine painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Gentile da Fabriano
bio: Gentile da Fabriano was an Italian painter associated with Italian Renaissance & International Gothic. Active during the late medieval period, the supreme master of the International Gothic style in Italy, Gentile da Fabriano created sumptuous altarpieces with luminous gold grounds, intricate detail, and elegant courtly figures. His Adoration of the Magi is one of the most splendid achievements of early Renaissance painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Pinturicchio
bio: Pinturicchio was an Italian painter associated with Italian Renaissance & Umbrian school. Active during the late medieval period, an extraordinarily prolific Umbrian Renaissance painter, Pinturicchio decorated the Borgia Apartments in the Vatican and the Piccolomini Library in Siena with vibrant, narrative fresco cycles. His decorative flair, rich ornamentation, and love of pageantry make his work the epitome of Renaissance decorative painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Cosimo Tura
bio: Cosimo Tura was an Italian painter associated with Italian Renaissance & Ferrarese school. Active during the late medieval period, the founder of the Ferrarese school of painting, Tura developed a distinctive, hard-edged, intensely expressive style characterized by sculptural drapery, fantastical landscapes, and acid colours. His unconventional, visionary approach to religious painting makes him one of the most original artists of the 15th century. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Francesco del Cossa
bio: Francesco del Cossa was an Italian painter associated with Italian Renaissance & Ferrarese school. Active during the late medieval period, a master of the Ferrarese school, Cossa contributed the magnificent frescoes of the Months in the Palazzo Schifanoia, among the most complex allegorical cycles of the Renaissance. His powerful figures, intricate iconography, and brilliant colour define the idiosyncratic Ferrarese Renaissance style. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Federico Barocci
bio: Federico Barocci was an Italian painter associated with Italian Renaissance & Mannerism & Baroque. Active during the sixteenth century, the preeminent Italian painter of the late 16th century, Barocci developed a deeply personal style blending Correggio's sfumato with Venetian colour and a tender, naturalistic piety. His luminous, emotionally resonant religious works profoundly influenced the early Baroque in both Italy and northern Europe. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Daniele da Volterra
bio: Daniele da Volterra was an Italian painter associated with Italian Renaissance & Mannerism. Active during the sixteenth century, a prominent Mannerist painter and sculptor, Daniele da Volterra was a close associate of Michelangelo and is best known for his fresco The Deposition in the Orsini Chapel. His controversial task of painting loincloths on Michelangelo's Last Judgment figures gave him the nickname Il Braghettone. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Jean-Baptiste Oudry
bio: Jean-Baptiste Oudry was a French painter associated with Rococo & Still life & Animal painting. Active during the eighteenth century, the preeminent French still-life and animal painter of the Rococo period, Oudry served as court painter to Louis XV and director of the Beauvais tapestry factory. His sumptuous still lifes of game, flowers, and fruit, rendered with almost trompe-l'œil precision, are masterpieces of the genre. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Félix Ziem
bio: Félix Ziem was a French painter associated with Barbizon school & Orientalism. Active during the nineteenth century, a peripatetic French painter associated with the Barbizon school and Orientalism, Ziem travelled extensively through the Ottoman Empire and painted luminous views of the Bosphorus, Venice, and Provence. His vibrant, freely painted harbour scenes and sun-drenched landscapes were enormously popular in his lifetime. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Eugène Fromentin
bio: Eugène Fromentin was a French painter associated with Orientalism & Romanticism. Active during the nineteenth century, a remarkable double talent as painter and writer, Fromentin produced vivid Orientalist scenes of North African horsemen, falconry, and desert life based on his travels in Algeria. His novel Dominique and art criticism rank him among the most perceptive French cultural commentators of the 19th century. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Henri-Joseph Harpignies
bio: Henri-Joseph Harpignies was a French painter associated with Barbizon school & Landscape painting. Active during the nineteenth century, a master of the Barbizon landscape tradition who lived to ninety-seven, Harpignies painted serene, structurally solid views of the French countryside, especially the Nivernais and the Allier valley. His disciplined compositions and subtle harmonies bridged the Barbizon school and later landscape practices. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Achille-Etna Michallon
bio: Achille-Etna Michallon was a French painter associated with Neoclassicism & Landscape painting. Active during the nineteenth century, a promising French landscape painter who won the Prix de Rome for historical landscape and died tragically at twenty-six. Michallon's rigorous classical compositions and refined sense of atmosphere influenced his most famous pupil, Camille Corot, and the development of French landscape painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Richard Dadd
bio: Richard Dadd was a British painter associated with Victorian painting & Fairy painting. Active during the nineteenth century, a brilliantly original British painter of fairy subjects and Orientalist scenes who spent most of his career confined to Bethlem and Broadmoor hospitals after killing his father. His meticulously detailed masterpiece The Fairy Fellers' Master-Stroke was painted entirely in the asylum and is an unique vision of Victorian fantasy. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: William Etty
bio: William Etty was a British painter associated with British art & Romanticism & History painting. Active during the nineteenth century, the foremost British painter of the nude in the 19th century, Etty produced sensuous, richly coloured mythological and historical scenes inspired by Titian and Rubens. Despite persistent critical controversy over his subject matter, his virtuoso handling of flesh tones and his influence on the British school were immense. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Benjamin Robert Haydon
bio: Benjamin Robert Haydon was a British painter associated with British art & Romanticism & History painting. Active during the nineteenth century, a bombastic and tragic figure of British Romantic history painting, Haydon created vast canvases of heroic subjects but struggled perpetually with debt and critical rejection. His passionate advocacy for state patronage of art and his autobiographical writings, including his suicide note, are as famous as his paintings. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Edward John Poynter
bio: Edward John Poynter was a British painter associated with British art & Academic art & Classicism. Active during the nineteenth century, a leading British academic classicist and President of the Royal Academy, Poynter produced impeccably finished historical and mythological paintings with archaeologically precise detail. His grand composition Israel in Egypt and his directorship of the National Gallery shaped late-Victorian artistic taste. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Wilhelm Trübner
bio: Wilhelm Trübner was a German painter associated with German art & Realism & Munich School. Active during the nineteenth century, a leading German Realist painter associated with the Munich School and the Leibl Circle, Trübner developed a bold, painterly style emphasizing colour and form over narrative. His portraits, still lifes, and landscapes with their vigorous brushwork and unified tonal harmony anticipate German Expressionism. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Carl Gustav Carus
bio: Carl Gustav Carus was a German painter associated with German Romanticism & Landscape painting. Active during the nineteenth century, a physician, philosopher, and painter, Carus was a leading theorist of German Romantic landscape painting and a close friend of Caspar David Friedrich. His symbolic, emotionally charged landscapes, coupled with his writings on the Erdlebenbild theory, made him a key figure of Dresden Romanticism. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Georg Friedrich Kersting
bio: Georg Friedrich Kersting was a German painter associated with German Romanticism & Biedermeier. Active during the nineteenth century, the leading German Biedermeier interior painter, Kersting created intimate, meticulously detailed portrayals of domestic life and studio interiors that epitomize the bourgeois Biedermeier sensibility. His paintings of Caspar David Friedrich in his studio and Man by Lamplight are delicate masterpieces of quiet observation. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Ludwig Richter
bio: Ludwig Richter was a German painter associated with German Romanticism & Biedermeier. Active during the nineteenth century, One of the most beloved German painters and illustrators of the Biedermeier period, Richter created idyllic, deeply sentimental images of German family life, folk tales, and village scenes. His woodcut illustrations for children's books and his warm, nostalgic paintings shaped the popular visual imagination of 19th-century Germany. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Anton Graff
bio: Anton Graff was a Swiss painter associated with Neoclassicism & Portrait painting. Active during the eighteenth century, the most important portrait painter in 18th-century Germany and Switzerland, Graff portrayed the leading intellectual and cultural figures of the Enlightenment, including Lessing, Schiller, and Herder. His psychologically penetrating, naturalistic portraits established a new standard of realism in German-speaking Europe. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Joachim Patinir
bio: Joachim Patinir was a Netherlandish painter associated with Netherlandish Renaissance & Landscape painting. Active during the sixteenth century, the pioneering Netherlandish landscape painter who established the world landscape genre, Patinir invented vast panoramic vistas with towering rock formations, winding rivers, and tiny Biblical figures. His imaginative, topographical landscapes influenced Pieter Bruegel the Elder and founded landscape painting as an independent genre in Europe. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Hendrick Goltzius
bio: Hendrick Goltzius was a Dutch painter associated with Northern Mannerism. Active during the sixteenth century, the most celebrated Dutch printmaker and draftsman of the late 16th century, Goltzius also produced remarkable oil paintings and chiaroscuro woodcuts in a virtuosic Mannerist style. His technical brilliance, especially in his pen works and his later more naturalistic portraits, places him among the greatest Netherlandish artists. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Maarten van Heemskerck
bio: Maarten van Heemskerck was a Netherlandish painter associated with Netherlandish Renaissance & Mannerism. Active during the sixteenth century, a leading Netherlandish painter of the 16th century, Heemskerck studied in Italy and introduced Italianate Mannerist motifs to Haarlem. His prolific output of altarpieces, portraits, and prints, together with his detailed drawings of Roman antiquities, made him a pivotal figure between Netherlandish traditions and the Renaissance. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Jan van Scorel
bio: Jan van Scorel was a Netherlandish painter associated with Netherlandish Renaissance & Romanism. Active during the sixteenth century, a pioneering Netherlandish Romanist painter who travelled to Italy and Jerusalem, Scorel brought a thorough understanding of Italian Renaissance composition and antique motifs back to Utrecht. His portraits and altarpieces, distinguished by monumental figures and classical architectural settings, transformed Netherlandish painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Nicolaes Maes
bio: Nicolaes Maes was a Dutch painter associated with Dutch Golden Age. Active during the seventeenth century, a leading Dutch Golden Age painter who studied under Rembrandt in Amsterdam, Maes initially excelled in intimate domestic genre scenes before turning to elegant portraiture in his later career. His warmly lit interiors with mothers and children and his refined, flattering portraits epitomize two distinct phases of Dutch 17th-century painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Nikolaos Gyzis
bio: Nikolaos Gyzis was a Greek painter associated with Greek art & Munich School & Academic art. Active during the nineteenth century, the most celebrated Greek painter of the 19th century and a leading figure of the Munich School, Gyzis created allegorical works, genre scenes, and portraits imbued with a gentle, poetic realism. His masterpiece The Secret School, depicting clandestine Greek education under Ottoman rule, became a powerful national symbol. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Georgios Iakovidis
bio: Georgios Iakovidis was a Greek painter associated with Greek art & Munich School & Academic art. Active during the nineteenth century, the first director of the National Gallery of Greece and a leading Munich School painter, Iakovidis specialized in tender portraits of children and grandparents in domestic interiors. His warm, naturalistic depictions of childhood innocence and old age are among the most beloved works of modern Greek painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Konstantinos Volanakis
bio: Konstantinos Volanakis was a Greek painter associated with Greek art & Marine painting & Realism. Active during the nineteenth century, the founder of modern Greek marine painting, Volanakis studied in Munich and Venice and devoted his career to depicting the Aegean Sea, harbours, and naval battles with luminous realism. His panoramic seascapes and coastal scenes established him as the most important Greek painter of maritime subjects. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Osman Hamdi Bey
bio: Osman Hamdi Bey was an Ottoman painter associated with Orientalism & Realism. Active during the nineteenth century, Ottoman administrator, archaeologist and painter who founded the İstanbul Archaeology Museum and directed the Ottoman Imperial School of Fine Arts. His Orientalist canvases like The Tortoise Trainer reflect a synthesis of Western academic technique with Ottoman cultural themes. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Şeker Ahmet Paşa
bio: Şeker Ahmet Paşa was an Ottoman painter associated with Realism & Naturalism. Active during the nineteenth century, Ottoman military painter and art educator trained in Paris under Gustave Boulanger and Jean-Léon Gérôme, known for his precise naturalist landscapes, still lifes, and forest scenes. He was a leading figure in the introduction of Western painting techniques to the Ottoman world. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Hoca Ali Rıza
bio: Hoca Ali Rıza was an Ottoman painter associated with Impressionism & Landscape Painting. Active during the nineteenth century, Turkish painter and art educator known for his impressionistic Istanbul cityscapes, naval scenes, and landscapes documenting the late Ottoman capital. He served as president of the Ottoman Society of Painters and taught at the Naval Academy. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Mihri Müşfik Hanım
bio: Mihri Müşfik Hanım was a Turkish painter associated with Portraiture & Impressionism. Active during the early twentieth century, First female Turkish painter of the Ottoman era, known for her expressive portraits of Istanbul high society and intellectual figures. She studied in Rome and Paris, later taught at the İstanbul Academy of Fine Arts. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Halil Paşa
bio: Halil Paşa was an Ottoman painter associated with Impressionism & Landscape Painting. Active during the nineteenth century, Ottoman military painter and impressionist noted for his luminous landscapes, garden scenes, and views of the Bosphorus. Trained in Paris under Jean-Léon Gérôme, he blended French plein-air technique with Turkish subject matter. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Kamal-ol-Molk
bio: Kamal-ol-Molk was an Iranian painter associated with Realism & Qajar Art. Active during the nineteenth century, Most celebrated Iranian painter of the Qajar era, court painter to Naser al-Din Shah Qajar, whose meticulously detailed portraits and landscapes set the standard for Persian academic realism. He later founded the Kamal-ol-Molk Academy of Fine Arts. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Abu'l-Hasan Ghaffari
bio: Abu'l-Hasan Ghaffari was an Iranian painter associated with Qajar Art & Realism. Active during the nineteenth century, Leading Persian painter of the Qajar court, known as Sani' al-Molk, who pioneered the integration of European naturalist techniques into Persian miniature and portrait traditions. His illustrated manuscript of One Thousand and One Nights is a landmark of Persian book painting. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Juan Luna
bio: Juan Luna was a Filipino painter associated with Romanticism & Academic Art. Active during the nineteenth century, Filipino painter, sculptor and revolutionary whose masterpiece Spoliarium won gold at the 1884 Madrid Exposition, making him the first Filipino to gain international recognition. His epic historical canvases blended Spanish academic style with nationalist themes. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Félix Resurrección Hidalgo
bio: Félix Resurrección Hidalgo was a Filipino painter associated with Romanticism & Academic Art. Active during the nineteenth century, Filipino painter of the Propaganda Movement whose allegorical works La Barca de Aqueronte and Las Vírgenes Cristianas Expuestas al Populacho won international acclaim in Spain. He was a key figure in the Filipino artistic diaspora in Europe. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Rodolfo Amoedo
bio: Rodolfo Amoedo was a Brazilian painter associated with Academic Art & Symbolism. Active during the nineteenth century, Brazilian painter, designer and professor whose works O Último Tamoio and A Partida da Estação Central bridged academic classicism and emerging symbolism. He taught at the National School of Fine Arts and influenced generations of Brazilian painters. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Cándido López
bio: Cándido López was an Argentine painter associated with Realism & Naïve Art. Active during the nineteenth century, Argentine painter and soldier who lost his right arm in the Paraguayan War and learned to paint with his left hand to create an unique visual chronicle of the conflict. His panoramic battle scenes combine naive perspective with meticulous documentary detail. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Ángel Della Valle
bio: Ángel Della Valle was an Argentine painter associated with Realism & Costumbrismo. Active during the nineteenth century, Argentine painter known for his large-scale costumbrist paintings of gaucho traditions and rural Argentine life, most famously La Vuelta del Malón. His works document the vanishing lifestyles of the Argentine pampas with romantic realism. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: José Guadalupe Posada
bio: José Guadalupe Posada was a Mexican painter associated with Popular Art & Satire. Active during the nineteenth century, Mexican printmaker and illustrator whose satirical calavera (skeleton) imagery, most famously La Catrina, became an enduring symbol of Mexican popular art and Day of the Dead iconography. His broadsides critiqued Porfirian society and influenced later Mexican muralists. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: John Peter Russell
bio: John Peter Russell was an Australian painter associated with Impressionism & Post-Impressionism. Active during the nineteenth century, Australian impressionist painter, friend of Van Gogh, Monet and Rodin, whose vibrant landscapes painted on Belle Île anticipate fauvist color. Though largely forgotten for decades, he is now recognized as one of the most expressive Australian painters of his generation. His artistic legacy continues to be preserved and studied in major museums around the world, and his influence on subsequent generations of painters remains widely acknowledged by art historians.

name: Rupert Bunny
bio: Rupert Bunny was an Australian painter associated with Symbolism & Impressionism. Active during the nineteenth century, Australian painter who spent most of his career in France, producing elegant symbolist and impressionist works depicting women in garden settings, mythological scenes, and modern Parisian life. He was one of the few Australian painters to achieve success at the Paris Salon.
